title: Offline and online stores in a feature platform
description: How a feature store splits a batch offline store for training from a low-latency online store for serving, synced from one definition.
body: Use when deciding whether a feature store fits a workload. A feature store keeps a columnar offline store for point-in-time backfills and a key-value online store for millisecond reads, materialized from one feature definition to keep them consistent. Do not treat it as a generic cache — its value is the offline/online contract and point-in-time joins. Actions: model entities and features, configure materialization cadence, and confirm online freshness meets the serving SLO. Validation signals include matching offline/online values and an online read latency within budget. Risk boundary: a stale online store serves outdated features; if materialization lags the freshness SLO, escalate before relying on online reads.
